login.voiceCheckBody: After your PIN, you'll say a short passphrase so Bede knows it's really you.
login.quote: "The sole true end of education is simply this: to teach men how to teach themselves." — Dorothy L. Sayers, <italic>The Lost Tools of Learning</italic>
login.voiceNamePrompt: Enter your name for the voice check:

tutorSession.feedbackTooltip: Tell us what's working and what isn't
tutorSession.finishedLearningTime: {{name}}, you have finished your learning time for today.
tutorSession.eyeRestBreakBody: {{name}}, that's enough screen time for now.
tutorSession.eyeRestBreakDuration: This break is at least {{minutes}} minutes — step away from the screen.
tutorSession.breakTimeInstructions: Step away from the screen. Be with nature, rest your eyes, or spend a quiet moment with God.
tutorSession.summarySubtitle: Prepared by Bede · for your records
tutorSession.unableToGenerateSummary: Unable to generate summary — check your API connection.
tutorSession.sentTo: Sent to {{email}}. This address wasn't saved anywhere.
tutorSession.emailDisclaimer: An informal impression from today's session, not an official evaluation. Used once to send this email, never stored, and never shown to your student.

chat.drawingReady: Drawing ready. Add a note, or just send it.
chat.uploadNarration: Upload narration from your notebook (e.g. inq)
chat.voiceModeOnTapOff: Voice mode on. Tap to turn off.
chat.placeholderOnBreak: On a break. Bede will be here when you return.
chat.placeholderVoiceModeWaiting: Voice mode on. Waiting for Bede…
chat.placeholderTypeOrMic: Type or tap the mic to speak…
chat.placeholderShareThoughts: Share your thoughts or answer Bede's question…
chat.hintVoiceModeOn: Voice mode on. Just speak, and Bede will hear and reply automatically. Tap the mic to turn it off.
chat.hintEnterOrMic: Enter to send · mic for voice input
chat.hintEnterOnly: Press Enter to send · Shift+Enter for new line
chat.nextSubjectFallback: Let's move on to our next subject!

breakActivities: Look at something at least 20 feet away for 20 seconds — it gives your eyes a real rest.
Step outside if you can. Find one living thing, like a bird or a leaf, and really look at it for a minute.
Take a quiet moment to thank God for one thing you learned or noticed today.
Do 15 jumping jacks or stretch your arms up over your head.
Look out a window at the sky. What are the clouds doing right now? Which way is the wind blowing?
Mental math: what is 17 + 26? Try it without writing it down.
Pray a slow Our Father, paying attention to one phrase you usually rush past.
Name five animals whose names start with the letter B.
Find something outside worth drawing later. Remember it for your nature notebook.
Walk to another room and back before you sit down again.
Close your eyes gently and roll your shoulders back ten times.
Sit still for one quiet minute. God speaks in the silence. Just listen.
Mental math: what is 9 x 7? Say the answer out loud.
Think of a word that rhymes with "light" — how many can you find?
Step outside or to a window and take five slow, deep breaths.
Mental math: if you have 3 dozen eggs, how many eggs is that?
